content: The learning-focused lexical definition of reflexion (as a core term for metacognitive reflection in high school English writing curricula) can be used to ground the design of better LLM self-reflection mechanisms by framing LLM self-reflection as a structured, iterative writing workshop rather than just a computational inconsistency check. Just as students learn to improve their arguments by revising drafts based on self-reflection on their own writing, LLMs can be trained to follow the same structured metacognitive routine that human learners use to refine output.
